brief.name: MERIDIAN
brief.industry: Nonprofit climate-research org publishing a scroll-driven data story on ocean warming
brief.audience: Educated general public, journalists, and policy staffers who skim first and read deeply only if hooked. They distrust alarmism and respond to credible, well-sourced data. Most read on mobile during commutes.
brief.tone: literate, measured, urgent-but-credible, evidence-led, humane, unhurried
brief.references: NYT/The Pudding scrollytelling features, Reuters graphics, Our World in Data charts, Edward Tufte sparklines, Kurzgesagt restraint (without the cartoon)
brief.context: Open with a one-line intro hook over a single striking visual. Three sticky chapters each pin a chart while narrative text scrolls beside it: (1) sea-surface temperature since 1900, (2) coral bleaching events, (3) projected 2050 scenarios. Data reveals animate in as each chapter enters view. Include 2 pull-quotes from named scientists with affiliation. End with a clear conclusion CTA: 'Read the methodology' + 'Share this story'. Every chart needs an accessible text alternative and a visible data source. On reduced-motion or no-JS, the charts must render statically in order with no pinning.
brief.avoid: Apocalyptic stock imagery. Motion for its own sake. Unsourced numbers. Decorative charts that don't answer a question. Hijacking the scrollbar so users can't control pace. Auto-playing video. SaaS-style feature-grid sections.
